decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at ~476 W/(m*K). Increasing this value towards the upper bound will improve lateral heat spreading and reduce the local hot spot.

changes.reason: Maximizing the spreader conductivity within bounds enhances lateral heat diffusion away from the chip, directly addressing the high severity temperature violation.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches the upper bound of 500.0
If this single change is insufficient, subsequent steps may require increasing spreader geometry dimensions
